summary: Round-2 fixes close all three round-1 findings cleanly: UUID-based filenames remove the listFiles().size race (T17-R1), writeAttachmentStaging now takes filesDir explicitly so the containment check is no longer tautological (T17-R3), and pruneStagingDir + attachment_only_first_persisted snackbar cover the N>1 image + in-flight remove race (T17-R2 / T17-S4). Security improvements (logged partial-write cleanup failures, savePersistentAttachment containment check, suspend writeAttachmentPayload) are all in place. Tests are thorough and assert the right invariants. Two minor observations below; none block approval.

suggestions.suggestion: pruneStagingDir + commitDraftChat are two serial IO calls inside a single viewModelScope.launch. A user tapping addImageBitmap() between send() and the prune coroutine's turn (the send gate only flips isGenerating=true in _uiState — it doesn't reject add* calls) would launch a fresh stageDraftAttachmentIfNeeded coroutine targeting the same draftStagingDir. Depending on scheduling this new staged file can land (a) before prune runs — it would be deleted by prune as not-in-retain, (b) after prune but before rename — it would become an orphan inside the final attachments/{chatId}/ dir that no sweep catches, or (c) after rename — it would recreate the now-absent .staging-* dir for startup sweep to collect. In practice MultimodalInputBar disables the add-attachment controls when isGenerating=true, so this is a defense-in-depth concern rather than a live bug. Consider gating addImages/addImageBitmap/addAudio on uiState.isGenerating (or a dedicated committing flag) at the VM level so the invariant is enforced without relying on UI composition.
suggestions.benefit: Removes a latent orphan-file race that the UI currently masks; the VM's correctness would no longer depend on a specific composable's disabled-state.

suggestions.suggestion: In runInferencePersistent the attachment_only_first_persisted snackbar fires when pending.count { it is Attachment.Image } > 1. The Persistent path actually supports one persisted image AND one persisted audio per USER row (savePersistentAttachment is called twice — lines 809-816). The current threshold catches the multi-image MVP limitation but not a multi-audio case; today addAudio rejects a second audio clip upfront (line 451), so the asymmetry is safe today. When a future phase admits multiple audio attachments this snackbar condition will need updating. A small comment at line 785 stating 'audio is UI-gated to a single clip, so only images need the N>1 warning here' would make the invariant explicit for future maintainers.
suggestions.benefit: Documents a cross-file invariant (addAudio gate ↔ persist-one MVP limit) so the next contributor to the audio path doesn't miss the snackbar contract.

suggestions.suggestion: pruneStagingDir silently swallows individual delete failures after logging — appropriate per the KDoc (a single orphan can't abort the commit). Consider including the retain-set size in the log message ('failed to delete {path} during prune (retained=N)') to make post-incident diagnosis easier when the startup sweep later reports leftover files.
suggestions.benefit: Tighter breadcrumb trail when forensics are needed on the staging → persistent transition.

T17-R1_filename_race.evidence: DefaultChatRepository.collisionFreeFilename (lines 265-271) returns img_{UUID}.png / audio_{UUID}.wav. Used by both writeAttachmentStaging (line 160) and savePersistentAttachment (line 213). Regression test writeAttachmentStaging_concurrentWrites_uniqueFilenames (ChatRepositoryTest lines 343-362) asserts 5 parallel writes all get unique filenames. savePersistentAttachment_concurrentCalls_uniqueFilenames (lines 412-423) asserts the same for the persistent path.

T17-R2_multi_image_drop.evidence: attachment_only_first_persisted string added (strings.xml:82). ChatViewModel.commitDraft emits it when imageCount > 1 (line 697-704) and runInferencePersistent emits it when pending.count { it is Attachment.Image } > 1 (line 785-790). ChatRepository.pruneStagingDir (interface lines 104-108, impl DefaultChatRepository lines 181-197) deletes unreferenced staged files. commitDraft calls it with retain = setOfNotNull(stagedImage, stagedAudio) (line 722-726) BEFORE commitDraftChat. Test draftMode_multipleImages_warnAndPruneExtrasBeforeCommit (ChatViewModelTest lines 675-701) asserts both the snackbar and the single-file commit.

T17-R3_derivation.evidence: ChatRepository.writeAttachmentStaging signature (line 75-79) takes filesDir: File explicitly; DefaultChatRepository impl (lines 145-164) calls requireInsideAttachmentsRoot(stagingDir, filesDir) directly. No parentFile?.parentFile derivation remains. ChatViewModel.stageDraftAttachmentIfNeeded passes context.filesDir (line 475, 478). FakeChatRepository.writeAttachmentStaging signature matches.

T17-S3_partial_write_logging.evidence: writeAttachmentPayload is now `private suspend fun` (DefaultChatRepository line 233); the catch block at line 243-255 logs delete-of-partial-file failures via errorLog.e("attachment-save", ...) before rethrowing the original cause. Test writeAttachmentStaging_failedWrite_rollsBackPartialFile (ChatRepositoryTest lines 460-489) exercises the path.

T17-S4_prune_orphans.evidence: Combined with T17-R2 via pruneStagingDir. Retain-set is built from the USER row's actual stagedImageFilename/stagedAudioFilename — exactly the files commitDraftChat will reference.

T17-T1_delete_through_repository.evidence: ChatRepository.deleteStagedAttachment added (interface lines 88-92). ChatViewModel.deleteStagedFileIfAny (lines 516-534) routes through chatRepository.deleteStagedAttachment. FakeChatRepository.deletedStagedFiles records the filename. Test draftMode_removeAttachment_deletesStagedFile (ChatViewModelTest lines 583-604) asserts the list equals ["img_0.png"].

T17-T2_partial_write_rollback.evidence: ChatRepositoryTest.writeAttachmentStaging_failedWrite_rollsBackPartialFile (lines 460-489) asserts no extra files appear under attachments root when the write throws.

T17-T3_persistent_containment.evidence: DefaultChatRepository.savePersistentAttachment calls requireInsideAttachmentsRoot(chatDir, filesDir) at line 209 BEFORE mkdirs. Test savePersistentAttachment_chatDirOutsideRoot_throws (ChatRepositoryTest lines 425-457) covers the mismatched-filesDir path.

inFlightRaceAnalysis.scenario: stageDraftAttachmentIfNeeded launch lands between pruneStagingDir and commitDraftChat rename
inFlightRaceAnalysis.verdict: Not a regression from this task; pre-existing pattern. The send()-gate blocks via `pending.any { it.isStagingPending() }` — any attachment captured in `pending` with stagedFilename=null aborts the send. Because `pending = _attachments.value` is read synchronously at send(), any NEW addImageBitmap call after send() returns adds to _attachments AFTER the snapshot, so its in-flight stage write is not referenced by stagedImage/stagedAudio and would be pruned (if on-disk before prune) or orphan-leaked (if on-disk after prune but before rename). In production the attachment bar is UI-gated by isGenerating=true. Suggested as minor (see suggestion 1) — VM-level gating would harden defense-in-depth but is not required for the round-2 approval.

snackbarVerification.draft_commitDraft_placement: synchronous in commitDraft before viewModelScope.launch (line 697-704). Fires on user tap so UI sees it immediately — correct.
snackbarVerification.persistent_runInferencePersistent_placem: synchronous in runInferencePersistent before viewModelScope.launch (line 785-790). Same pattern. Correct.
snackbarVerification.wording: Сохранено только первое фото — остальные прикреплены к ответу модели, но не попадут в историю. Clear and user-facing-appropriate; explains both the 'only first persisted' limit AND that the extras still reach inference.
